text.title: react-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for feature-based React module structure.

rule.statement: Organise code under src/features/<feature-name>/. Each feature folder owns components/, hooks/, api.ts, types.ts. Shared primitives go to src/components/ui/; truly shared hooks to src/hooks/.
rule.rationale: Feature cohesion reduces cross-folder jumps for any single change. File-type-only layouts scatter the cost of touching one feature across N directories.

rule.statement: Components are named function declarations: `export function Card(props: CardProps) {}`. Anonymous arrow defaults (`export default () => …`) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Anonymous components show as "Unknown" in DevTools and stack traces. Named declarations are hoisted (importable before they appear in the file) and give every error a recognisable origin.

rule.statement: Every component declares a named interface for its props: `interface FooProps { … }`. Inline `(props: { … })` signatures are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Named interfaces enable rename refactors, IDE hover docs, and external consumers importing the type. Inline shapes are invisible at the consumer site.

rule.statement: Initialise React Context with `null`. The consumer hook throws if the value is null with a message naming the missing provider.
rule.rationale: A fake default value silently masks "consumer outside provider" bugs. Null + throw surfaces the bug at first call.

rule.statement: The Provider's `value` prop is built by useMemo, including every callback (each wrapped in useCallback). Inline `value={{ …state }}` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: An inline object value reference changes every render, forcing every consumer to re-render even when nothing they care about changed. Memoization keeps the reference stable.

rule.statement: Route each state slice exactly once: server state → TanStack Query/SWR; form state → React Hook Form; global UI → Zustand/Jotai; local → useState/useReducer; cross-feature shared → Context (only when external store is not in use).
rule.rationale: Each state class has different invalidation, caching, and lifecycle needs. Mixing them in one store produces accidental coupling and stale-cache bugs.
